tr.td: Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Erythrocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood by Automated count
MCV [Entitic mean volume] in Red Blood Cells by Automated count
MCHC [Entitic Mass/volume] in Red Blood Cells by Automated count
Erythrocyte [DistWidth] in Blood by Automated count
Platelets [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Urea nitrogen [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Aspartate aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Arterial blood
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
INR in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay
aPTT in Blood by Coagulation assay

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows evidence of renal dysfunction (elevated creatinine), cholestasis or possible liver dysfunction (markedly elevated total bilirubin, mildly elevated ALT), mild protein deficiency, and coagulopathy (prolonged aPTT, elevated INR). There is also mild metabolic acidosis (low pH, normal bicarbonate), increased red cell distribution width, and mild thrombocytopenia. Further clinical correlation and evaluation for underlying hepatic and renal pathology are recommended.

text.div: Provider Organization: Ospedale Vecchio di Montebelluna
